scope: AgenC automatic memory extraction after natural terminal turns. The matrix locks terminal-turn scheduling, successful direct-write bypass, memory-directory path policy, bounded manifest scanning, prompt construction, child-run coalescing, and durable saved-memory reporting.

approvedExceptions.reason: These files use TypeScript top-type annotations for decoded tool input and test fakes; the matrix rows remain required.

requiredBehaviors: executeExtractMemories accepts a snapshot of model-visible messages, completed tool results, turn context, and session
terminal commit wiring schedules extraction only after a natural terminal turn and nonblocking stop-hook result
main-agent gating excludes subagent sessions, including legacy string-form cli_subagent sources, and nested turn contexts before resolving a memory directory
run-turn drains pending extraction work after the terminal event and before the generator completes
assistant Write, Edit, or MultiEdit calls bypass child extraction only when their completed result has isError different from true and their main-agent path is absolute inside the memory directory
relative main-agent Write, Edit, or MultiEdit paths remain project-cwd-relative and do not bypass child extraction
failed direct writes route through the child extraction path and keep the visible-message cursor unchanged
saved-memory callbacks exclude the memory index file and report concrete markdown files written by the child
memory path resolution fails closed for unsafe explicit overrides, remote sessions without a mounted memory directory, and invalid trusted settings values

project-root memory directories use AgenC config-home project keys when no trusted override is present, with shared sanitizer behavior including leading hyphens, 200-character truncation, and hash suffixes
linked worktrees resolve to the canonical owning repository root before the automatic project key is computed
child tool policy rewrites FileRead, Grep, Glob, Edit, MultiEdit, and Write inputs into the memory directory and denies paths outside that directory
Glob policy checks both the resolved path and static pattern base so relative traversal and absolute outside patterns are denied
manifest scanning reads markdown frontmatter for description and memory type while ignoring the memory index file
manifest traversal is bounded by file count, depth, byte count, and line count, and ignores symlink entries before reading content
prompt construction advertises only FileRead, Grep, Glob, Edit, MultiEdit, and Write, with all access restricted to the memory directory
concurrent extraction requests coalesce behind one active child run per session and memory directory and retain the newest trailing context for a second pass
extraction lane state is bounded with idle-lane cleanup so completed sessions and project paths do not accumulate forever
drainPendingExtraction waits for all active extraction promises or times out after its caller-provided budget
child launch uses full-history fork mode with the exact extraction snapshot and triggering abort signal
child external abort listeners are removed during run-agent cleanup after completed or failed runs
memory extraction child launch runs in silent mode so parent mailbox notifications and child rollout records are suppressed
child launch preserves the parent tool catalog while enforcing memory-only access through child policy denial metadata
child policy denial metadata and failed child writes prevent cursor advancement so the same visible messages can be retried
